reviews.review_description:
Prepare To Dive takes you on a contemplative and interactive micro-adventure. The atmosphere is as serene as the surface of the ocean, but full of surprises beneath the waves!
The mixed reality textures on your walls, the smooth mechanics, and the thrill of plugging each leak—it's all there.
I was captivated by the immersive poetry of Prepare To Dive: your living room transforms into a century-old submarine, complete with flashing lights, valves, and virtual portholes to manipulate.
All the magic is there: repairing leaks, inspecting a shipwreck in the dim light, repelling a pirate during a storm, or battling crabs and Krakens!
Beagle Reality has created a simple yet effective sensory experience. Orientation, suspense, and repairs flow naturally. The whole experience is well-paced: exploration, maintenance, leaks, and mini-battles. Technically, it ran smoothly on Quest 3 during my testing.
A mini-adventure to escape into, and one that puts a smile on your face.
Gameplay Mechanics
The VR/MR interactions place you at the command station: detecting and repairing leaks, managing pressure, using a grappling hook to retrieve treasures, and defending the submersible. The spatial interface is intuitive, and the MR elements (portholes, pressure gauges attached to the wall or furniture) enhance the immersion.
￼It is good but I stopped playing after 20days because it was getting boring
Not bad, but the view outside isn't exactly Ocean Rift, more Amiga 500. A better, more interesting view would help. Exploring through, blue, kinda dull.
...but already a lot of fun in Mixed Reality! 🥰

Buy it. 

For $5, even in an early state this is a no Brainerd pickup. There are so few quality mixed reality games out currently and this one offers so much.

Sea exploration- use a map and the front of the sub to traverse the sea amd find underwater islands, fish and other fun things out the sub window

Combat- not the best and could be improved but for $5 I'm surprised it had any at all. There were pirates, crabs and even an octopus I fought within the first 20 .inutes of the game. Overall really solid

Sub functions- grab treasure from the sea, fix holes with leaking water, drain floods from the sub and just mess around with the mr objects like apples amd cheese that are lying around. You can place the ship parts where you'd like around the room amd they all have different functions like the map wall and the engine bay.

Overall I'd give it a 9/10 and hope they keep updating and making more mr content because this is the kind of stuff I bought the quest 3 to play
Fun game! Really love how it takes advantage of the passthrough. The AR/XR is so much fun!!

Full Video Gameplay on my Youtube Channel

This is a great way to show case what mixed reality can do. Setup your space how you want it to build your submarine and then goto different islands and fight off enemies along the way.

The main problem is depending on your place space when you have to stop the water coming into your room it could be anywhere and usually behind some furniture that you cannot get to. Would be good if it was more random as it was in the same place everyday.

It is a fun experience and with different enemy types and collectables along the way. Only real issue was there isnt really any music and not enough sound effects when your fighting or doing things around your ship.

If you have a quest 3 check it out its very cheap and its a good game to show friends.

reviews.review_title: Totally worth the price of admission
reviews.review_description: This game is a very unique and fun use of mixed reality. It made me realize that I don’t really have any bare walls where I play. You can just alter where you place your walls. I can’t wait to see what’s next for this game.
Love this game! Crabs are really scary 😂

It was really solid and fun to see my room transformed in a Submarine really cool sure it’s early stage but fun to see holes to repair and my room full of water cool I hope in new mission for the price you can’t go wrong but it 👀👀😀
